title: Mastering Scroll Animations with GSAP ScrollTrigger
excerpt: A deep dive into creating buttery-smooth scroll-driven animations using GSAP's ScrollTrigger plugin — from basic reveals to complex parallax effects.
body:
Why GSAP?

CSS scroll-driven animations are getting better, but GSAP still offers unmatched control, easing options, and cross-browser consistency. ScrollTrigger makes it trivial to tie any animation to scroll position.

The Basics

A simple fade-up on scroll entry takes just a few lines:

gsap.from('.section', {
  y: 60,
  opacity: 0,
  scrollTrigger: {
    trigger: '.section',
    start: 'top 80%',
  }
});

Advanced Techniques

Pinned Horizontal Scroll

One of the most impressive effects is horizontal scrolling within a vertically scrolling page. The trick is pinning a container and translating its content based on scroll progress.

Parallax Layers

Creating depth with parallax is about moving elements at different speeds. I typically use 3-4 layers with scrub values between 0.5 and 2.

Performance Tips

  • Use will-change: transform sparingly — only during active animations
  • Prefer transform and opacity — they're GPU-accelerated
  • Call ScrollTrigger.refresh() after dynamic content loads
  • Use batch() for staggering many elements efficiently

Conclusion

GSAP + ScrollTrigger is the gold standard for scroll animations in 2026. The API is intuitive, the performance is excellent, and the creative possibilities are endless.
